- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Debug webinar and video hosting errors

content.text:
All 4 fixes are in place. Here's what was broken and what I fixed:

---

**Bug 1 — WebSocket handler was missing** (live streaming broken)
The server's WebSocket handler only tracked `userId → socket` for notifications, with zero logic for the `wr_*` messages that both WebinarStudio and WatchWebinar send. Added a full webinar room system: when the host joins, viewers join and are tracked per room, and all WebRTC signaling (offer/answer/ICE), chat, Q&A, reactions, hand-raising, CTAs, and polls now route correctly between host and viewers.

**Bug 2 — `GET /api/webinars/public/:code` didn't exist** (watch page showed blank/error)
`WatchWebinar.tsx` fetches `/api/webinars/public/${code}` to load the webinar, but only `/api/webinars/public` (no code, returns all public webinars) existed. Added the by-code route above the `:id` route.

**Bug 3 — `POST /api/webinars/:id/recording/upload` didn't exist** (recording save failed)
After stopping a recording in the studio, the file was POSTed to this route, but it wasn't registered. Added it using the existing `videoUpload` multer middleware — saves the file and creates a recording DB record.

**Bug 4 — Video file upload used a blob URL** (uploaded videos wouldn't persist)
Dropping a file in the "Upload File" tab created a browser-local `blob:` URL and stored that in the DB, which breaks as soon as the page closes. Now it immediately POSTs the file to the new `/api/upload/video` endpoint and uses the server path (`/uploads/filename.mp4`) as the stored URL.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive audit report.

## Video Marketing & Webinar Features Audit

### 1. **PlatformView.tsx - Tabs/Sections Structure**

**Webinars Section (activeId):**
- `webinars` - List and manage webinars
- `series` - Recurring webinar series
- `landing-pages` - Registration landing pages
- `crm` - Lead/attendee management
- `recordings` - Webinar recordings
- `email-sequences` - Post-webinar automation
- `analytics` - Webinar performance metrics
- `settings` - Global platform configuration

**Video Hosting Section (activeId):**
- `video-hosting` - Video library management
- `vsl-studio` - VSL (Video Sales Letter) builder
- `collections` - Organize videos into groups
- `vsl-library` - All VSLs view
- `video-analytics` - Video performance tracking
- `video-crm` - Video viewer data
- `player-settings` - Branding and embed options

---

### 2. **VideoHosting.tsx - Features Analysis**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Video upload (file drag-drop, URL paste) with `/api/upload/video` endpoint
- Video metadata: title, description, duration, category, thumbnail
- VSL vs Standard video type differentiation
- Lead gate toggle (enabled/disabled state persists via API)
- Progress bar manipulation for VSLs (presets: Slow Start, Steady, Fast Start, Custom)
- Video library filtering by type (All/VSLs/Standard)
- Video deletion, embed code generation with iframe/direct URL
- Watch rate visualization (fake data: calculated from video ID hash)

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Analytics: Watch rate, CTA clicks, engagement metrics all **hardcoded** based on video ID hash (`Math.round(40 + seed * 4.5)`)
- Total views counter: not populated from real data
- Average watch rate calculation: uses deterministic seed math, not actual viewer metrics
- VSL showcase retention curve, drop-off analytics, replay counts all **generated artificially**
- "Urgency text" and "Brand color" fields exist but only display placeholder values
- Captions and custom branding don't affect actual player rendering

**MISSING:**
- No `/watch-video/:id` route in App.tsx (embed code references it but page doesn't exist)
- No actual video playback page for hosted videos
- No real video analytics (views, completion rate, heatmaps)
- No lead capture on video play
- No A/B testing for thumbnails/CTAs
- No video trimming or editing
- No conversion tracking for hosted videos

---

### 3. **WebinarStudio.tsx - Features Analysis**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Live camera/screen sharing (getUserMedia API)
- Quality presets: 720p, 1080p, 4K
- Mic/camera toggle, screen share control
- Local recording (MediaRecorder → `/api/webinars/{id}/recording/upload`)
- WebRTC broadcasting (ICE servers configured, peer connection setup)
- Chat (host sends, viewers receive via WebSocket)
- Q&A panel (questions from attendees, mark answered)
- Attendees list with raised hands management
- Presenter notes (private, local to host)
- Polls: create, launch, live voting, end (WebSocket broadcast)
- CTA offer button (launch/hide live during stream)
- Waiting room with custom message/background video
- Broadcast URL field (HLS .m3u8 support via HLS.js)
- JIC (Just-In-Case) mode: plays pre-recorded replay video

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Attendee count shows live number but increments appear manually controlled
- Floating reactions (emoji) are local-only UI, don't track viewer sentiment
- CTA click count is incremented on WebSocket message but not stored
- Recording metadata (duration, title) sent to API but unclear if persisted
- Poll results display but no backend persistence check visible
- "Attendees" list appears to seed from registrations but hand-raising is simulated

**MISSING:**
- No actual RTMP/HLS streaming setup for large audiences (max ~50 viewers via WebRTC)
- No email notifications (registrations, reminders, replay links)
- No automatic recording backup/CDN upload
- No interactive features beyond polls/Q&A (whiteboards, breakout rooms)
- No speaker notes display for viewers
- No automatic transcription/captions
- No replay video processing/optimization
- No conversion pixel tracking for CTAs

---

### 4. **WatchWebinar.tsx - Viewer Experience**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Registration form (name, email → `/api/register/{code}`)
- Three phases: register → waiting → live → ended
- Waiting room with countdown timer (calculated from `scheduledAt`)
- Custom waiting room message/background video
- Live chat (send/receive via WebSocket)
- Q&A submission (private questions to host)
- Reactions (emoji floating animations, local-only)
- Hand raise toggle (sends message to host via WebSocket)
- Poll voting (real-time display, % bars updated on server message)
- Viewer count display
- Connection status indicator (Wifi/WifiLow icons)
- Static CTA banner (if `webferUrl`/`offerTitle` set)
- Live CTA overlay (host-triggered, clickable, dismissible)
- HLS broadcast URL support (via HLS.js)
- JIC playback (replay video for automated webinars)

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Viewer count increments artificially via WebSocket
- All CTA click tracking is client-side only (no backend conversion pixel)
- Poll vote counts are local-only until broadcast ends
- Replay not auto-generated (must be linked manually)

**MISSING:**
- No email follow-up after webinar
- No lead form capture before access (only name/email at registration)
- No video download permission check
- No session recording visibility
- No replay gating/expiry
- No affiliate link support
- No dynamic pricing in CTA

---

### 5. **Critical UI/Flow Gaps**

1. **No video player for `/watch-video/:id`** — All embed code points to non-existent route
2. **No real analytics backend** — All metrics are deterministic fakes
3. **Recording upload works but replay linking is manual** — No automatic processing
4. **CTA tracking is client-side only** — No server persistence of clicks
5. **No email infrastructure** — Registrations collected but no confirmations/reminders
6. **VSL features partially stubbed** — Progress bar config saved but not applied in player
7. **Lead gating UI exists but no form capture** — Only title/email at registration

**Status: ~40% real, ~60% demo/stubbed.** The platform demonstrates core video/webinar architecture but lacks production analytics, email workflows, and persistent conversion tracking.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive audit report.

## Video Marketing & Webinar Features Audit

### 1. **PlatformView.tsx - Tabs/Sections Structure**

**Webinars Section (activeId):**
- `webinars` - List and manage webinars
- `series` - Recurring webinar series
- `landing-pages` - Registration landing pages
- `crm` - Lead/attendee management
- `recordings` - Webinar recordings
- `email-sequences` - Post-webinar automation
- `analytics` - Webinar performance metrics
- `settings` - Global platform configuration

**Video Hosting Section (activeId):**
- `video-hosting` - Video library management
- `vsl-studio` - VSL (Video Sales Letter) builder
- `collections` - Organize videos into groups
- `vsl-library` - All VSLs view
- `video-analytics` - Video performance tracking
- `video-crm` - Video viewer data
- `player-settings` - Branding and embed options

---

### 2. **VideoHosting.tsx - Features Analysis**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Video upload (file drag-drop, URL paste) with `/api/upload/video` endpoint
- Video metadata: title, description, duration, category, thumbnail
- VSL vs Standard video type differentiation
- Lead gate toggle (enabled/disabled state persists via API)
- Progress bar manipulation for VSLs (presets: Slow Start, Steady, Fast Start, Custom)
- Video library filtering by type (All/VSLs/Standard)
- Video deletion, embed code generation with iframe/direct URL
- Watch rate visualization (fake data: calculated from video ID hash)

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Analytics: Watch rate, CTA clicks, engagement metrics all **hardcoded** based on video ID hash (`Math.round(40 + seed * 4.5)`)
- Total views counter: not populated from real data
- Average watch rate calculation: uses deterministic seed math, not actual viewer metrics
- VSL showcase retention curve, drop-off analytics, replay counts all **generated artificially**
- "Urgency text" and "Brand color" fields exist but only display placeholder values
- Captions and custom branding don't affect actual player rendering

**MISSING:**
- No `/watch-video/:id` route in App.tsx (embed code references it but page doesn't exist)
- No actual video playback page for hosted videos
- No real video analytics (views, completion rate, heatmaps)
- No lead capture on video play
- No A/B testing for thumbnails/CTAs
- No video trimming or editing
- No conversion tracking for hosted videos

---

### 3. **WebinarStudio.tsx - Features Analysis**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Live camera/screen sharing (getUserMedia API)
- Quality presets: 720p, 1080p, 4K
- Mic/camera toggle, screen share control
- Local recording (MediaRecorder → `/api/webinars/{id}/recording/upload`)
- WebRTC broadcasting (ICE servers configured, peer connection setup)
- Chat (host sends, viewers receive via WebSocket)
- Q&A panel (questions from attendees, mark answered)
- Attendees list with raised hands management
- Presenter notes (private, local to host)
- Polls: create, launch, live voting, end (WebSocket broadcast)
- CTA offer button (launch/hide live during stream)
- Waiting room with custom message/background video
- Broadcast URL field (HLS .m3u8 support via HLS.js)
- JIC (Just-In-Case) mode: plays pre-recorded replay video

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Attendee count shows live number but increments appear manually controlled
- Floating reactions (emoji) are local-only UI, don't track viewer sentiment
- CTA click count is incremented on WebSocket message but not stored
- Recording metadata (duration, title) sent to API but unclear if persisted
- Poll results display but no backend persistence check visible
- "Attendees" list appears to seed from registrations but hand-raising is simulated

**MISSING:**
- No actual RTMP/HLS streaming setup for large audiences (max ~50 viewers via WebRTC)
- No email notifications (registrations, reminders, replay links)
- No automatic recording backup/CDN upload
- No interactive features beyond polls/Q&A (whiteboards, breakout rooms)
- No speaker notes display for viewers
- No automatic transcription/captions
- No replay video processing/optimization
- No conversion pixel tracking for CTAs

---

### 4. **WatchWebinar.tsx - Viewer Experience**

**REAL/FUNCTIONAL:**
- Registration form (name, email → `/api/register/{code}`)
- Three phases: register → waiting → live → ended
- Waiting room with countdown timer (calculated from `scheduledAt`)
- Custom waiting room message/background video
- Live chat (send/receive via WebSocket)
- Q&A submission (private questions to host)
- Reactions (emoji floating animations, local-only)
- Hand raise toggle (sends message to host via WebSocket)
- Poll voting (real-time display, % bars updated on server message)
- Viewer count display
- Connection status indicator (Wifi/WifiLow icons)
- Static CTA banner (if `webferUrl`/`offerTitle` set)
- Live CTA overlay (host-triggered, clickable, dismissible)
- HLS broadcast URL support (via HLS.js)
- JIC playback (replay video for automated webinars)

**FAKE/STUBBED:**
- Viewer count increments artificially via WebSocket
- All CTA click tracking is client-side only (no backend conversion pixel)
- Poll vote counts are local-only until broadcast ends
- Replay not auto-generated (must be linked manually)

**MISSING:**
- No email follow-up after webinar
- No lead form capture before access (only name/email at registration)
- No video download permission check
- No session recording visibility
- No replay gating/expiry
- No affiliate link support
- No dynamic pricing in CTA

---

### 5. **Critical UI/Flow Gaps**

1. **No video player for `/watch-video/:id`** — All embed code points to non-existent route
2. **No real analytics backend** — All metrics are deterministic fakes
3. **Recording upload works but replay linking is manual** — No automatic processing
4. **CTA tracking is client-side only** — No server persistence of clicks
5. **No email infrastructure** — Registrations collected but no confirmations/reminders
6. **VSL features partially stubbed** — Progress bar config saved but not applied in player
7. **Lead gating UI exists but no form capture** — Only title/email at registration

**Status: ~40% real, ~60% demo/stubbed.** The platform demonstrates core video/webinar architecture but lacks production analytics, email workflows, and persistent conversion tracking.

Now I have a complete picture of what needs to be fixed. Let me:

1. Fix `VideoRow` - remove `seed` prop, remove fake `watchRate` and `ctaClicks`, show real views instead
2. Fix the stats header - remove fake `avgWatchRate`, replace with something real like number of lead-gated videos
3. Fix `VSLShowcase` - remove all fake seeded metrics (watchRate, engagementRate, ctaClicks, replays, dropOff, retention), use real data instead

For VSLShowcase, I need to fetch real data. 

The core issue is that I don't have per-video viewer sessions readily available in this component. I'll fetch real viewer session data for the selected VSL using `useQuery`, compute actual metrics from that data, and fall back to showing "—" when nothing's available. For `VideoRow`, I'll strip out the `seed` prop and fake computations, keeping just the real views count.

Looking at the video object structure, I see we have `views` but not `ctaClicks` at the row level without an extra API call per video. The cleanest approach is to remove the watch rate bar from `VideoRow` entirely since we can't compute real watch rate data there, and ditch the fake CTA clicks. In `VSLShowcase`, I'll fetch actual viewer sessions to calculate real completion percentage, average watch time, and CTA click counts from sessions where `ctaClicked` is true, while deciding whether to keep the retention curve as a visual or remove it since it's fabricated.

For the implementation: I'm stripping out the `seed` parameter from `VideoRow`, removing the watch rate bar for VSL videos and the CTA clicks display since we don't have that data at the listing level. I'm replacing the `avgWatchRate` stat in the header with a "Lead Gates" count to show how many videos have lead gates enabled. In `VSLShowcase`, I'm adding a query to fetch viewer sessions from the same endpoint the analytics tab uses, computing real stats from those sessions, and either showing actual values or a dash if data isn't available yet.

I realize we can't build a true per-decile retention curve from the session data we have—we only get the final `completionPct` and `watchedSeconds`, not the intermediate watch points. So I'll fetch the real viewer sessions and display actual metrics like average completion percentage and average watch time, with "No data" shown when there are no sessions.

Actually, I can approximate a retention curve by calculating what percentage of sessions watched past each 10% threshold—if a session has `completionPct >= 10`, it counts toward the 10% decile, and so on. This gives us a real retention curve from the completion data we do have. Let me implement this approach.

Computing the retention array...

Now I need to refactor the VideoRow component to remove the fake seed-based metrics and rely on actual session data instead—stripping out the artificial watch rate and CTA click calculations, though I'll need to figure out how to handle CTA clicks since we don't have per-video data for those.

The build should pass now.
